source_jobs.title: Customer Care Representative
source_jobs.description:
Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses—whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, users can easily manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, and run marketing campaigns via text and email. We also offer fully branded apps and websites, so studios can deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint.

We’ve raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It’s a unique moment to join us—we’re building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we’re looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Lovers of fitness and advocates for wellness businesses wanted! Our Customer Experience team is the first line of support for the studios, gyms, and wellness providers who run their business on Arketa. We answer their questions, solve real operational problems, and represent the voice of our Partners internally to shape the product. If you love building relationships, making people smile, and increasing access to wellness around the world, we want to meet you. Bonus points if you've already used Arketa (or a comparable platform) inside a wellness business yourself.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Customer Support Advocate, you'll act as the single point of contact for our Partners on inbound support inquiries, owning each interaction from first message to resolution. You'll guide Partners through Arketa's features, move quickly to creatively solve problems, and act as their internal advocate to influence our product decisions and roadmap. You'll address real operational problems for real wellness businesses — schedule issues, payment questions, migration blockers, integration hiccups — and deliver an exceptional experience that builds trust and loyalty to Arketa.

This is a fast-paced, high-volume support environment. You'll work across chat, email, and Zoom each day and manage multiple conversations in parallel.




HERE'S WHAT YOU'LL DO DAY-TO-DAY

 - Own each Partner interaction start to finish across chat, email, and scheduled Zoom calls — building trust, rapport, and partnership along the way.

 - Show up for your shift and your Partners. You'll work scheduled hours and stay available and engaged with the queue during that time. The pace is steady, the volume is real, and your Partners are counting on you being there when they reach out.

 - Own the resolution, not just the response. You're accountable for solving the Partner's problem the first time wherever possible — not just replying quickly. That means digging into the actual issue, confirming the fix landed, and closing the loop so the Partner doesn't have to come back for the same thing twice.

 - Think creatively, pivot fast, operate in ambiguity, and collaborate strategically across stakeholders. You'll problem-solve across the full surface area of the Arketa product, working with Product, Engineering, Onboarding, and Migrations to unblock Partners on issues that don't have a clean playbook yet.

 - Unlock the full power of Arketa for every Partner. Solve real operational problems, customize solutions to the way their business actually runs, and turn Partners into our biggest fans through expertise, empathy, and follow-through.

 - Become and stay an Arketa expert. Continuously learn new features, leverage AI-assisted internal tools to accelerate your research and drafting, and own the accuracy and Arketa voice of every response that goes out under your name.

 - Advocate for our Partners internally. Bring insights and industry feedback back to Product, Engineering, and CX leadership to improve features and prevent repeat contacts.
   
   


HERE'S WHAT WE'RE LOOKIN

source_jobs.title: Studio General Manager, Flagship
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ARKETA

Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses; whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, studios can manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, run automated marketing, and launch fully branded apps and websites. We make it simple for founders to deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint.

We’ve raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It’s a unique moment to join us. We’re building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we’re looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring a Studio General Manager to support the opening and ongoing success of a luxury social wellness club in New York City, as our Flagship to Arketa-powered studio owners.

What success looks like

We believe in setting our team up to thrive. Here's what your first year will look like:

First 30 days, Build the foundation: You'll hit the ground running by owning the studio launch from the ground up: partnering with our Ops leader about pre-sales and learning Arketa's platform inside and out. You'll also pitch in on a small portion of office management to get a feel for how we operate behind the scenes.

30–90 days - Build the business: Once the studio is live, the focus shifts to management and operations. You'll lead pre-sale membership planning, partner with our Lead Instructor to source and onboard instructors, and coordinate our soft launch — laying the groundwork for a thriving studio community.

6 months – 1 year, Run the studio: By this point, you'll be fully in the driver's seat. You'll manage a team that keeps the studio running day-to-day, coordinate instructor schedules, organize pop-up events and brand collaborations, and maintain a light load of office management responsibilities alongside it all.

This is a hands-on, hybrid role that blends:

 - Onsite Flagship studio management, leadership and hospitality

 - Office and environment management

You will play a critical role in launching and stabilizing a new studio. Our customers may visit our Flagship and you’ll be the go-to-expert on launching, scaling and managing a studio through our state of the art tech. This role is ideal for someone who deeply understands how studios actually run and enjoys helping others run them better.

You will be in the studio, in the tools, and in the details.




WHAT YOU’LL DO



STUDIO LEADERSHIP & GUEST EXPERIENCE

 - Act as a visible, calm, and professional leader on the studio floor

 - Set the standard for luxury hospitality, warmth, and operational excellence

 - Ensure every guest interaction reflects brand values and consistency

 - Support front-of-house teams with real-time coaching and guidance

 - Resolve guest issues with empathy, clarity, and strong judgment


SALES, MEMBERSHIP & BUSINESS SUPPORT

 - Support membership acquisition, conversion, and retention efforts

 - Reinforce consultative, education-first sales conversations

 - Ensure studio teams are aligned on offers, pricing, and promotions

 - Monitor daily operational and revenue indicators and flag issues early

 - Help studios improve utilization and consistency through better systems, not just effort


OPERATIONS, RELIABILITY & COMPLIANCE

 - Support scheduling, coverage planning, and shift readiness

 - Ensure accurate timekeeping and payroll inputs

 - Maintain operational standards across cleanliness, safety, and equipment

 - Document processes to reduce single-point dependency

 - Ensure compliance with NYC labor, safety, and operational requirements


OFFICE & ENVIRONMENT MANAGEMENT (5-10% OF YOUR ROLE)

 - Ensure the studio and office are

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer, CDMX
source_jobs.description:
Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses—whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, users can easily manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, and run marketing campaigns via text and email. We also offer fully branded apps and websites, so studios can deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint.

We’ve raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It’s a unique moment to join us—we’re building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we’re looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.


The Role:

Arketa is hiring a Full Stack Software Engineer who likes solving real problems for real customers. You will own work end to end, from reproducing an issue to shipping the fix, and you will grow into building full product features alongside our engineering team.



You will work across our Node.js and React/TypeScript stack with teammates based in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico.



This role is hybrid, 3 days per week in our Mexico City office. English fluency is required.



How this role works at Arketa:

We run a rotational ramp so that every engineer learns the product from the inside out before owning large features. We are upfront about this because it is core to how we ship confidently and deliver a great customer experience.

 - Months 0 to 9 (product mastery): You focus on fixing bugs and shipping small, high-impact features that come directly from customers and our support team. This is the fastest way to build deep, real-world knowledge of how Arketa works and where it breaks.

 - Month 9 and beyond (full feature ownership): You graduate into full feature development as a member of the product engineering team. If building out and leading is where your strengths are, there is room to help grow the function too.

This is not a support desk role. It is a software engineering role with a deliberate on-ramp that turns customer problems into product expertise.



Your first 90 days:

 - Days 0 to 30: Get familiar with the codebase and ramp up on bug fixes.

 - Days 30 to 90: Begin interacting with customers through our support channels and start building tools that improve bug triage.
   
   

Your first 6 to 9 months:

Roughly 60% of your time will be spent close to customers, fixing bugs and resolving user experience edge cases, with the other 40% focused on improving systems for the long term. In practice that looks like:

 - Triage, investigate, and resolve bugs reported by customers and the support team.

 - Own issues end to end: reproduce, diagnose, fix, validate, and communicate.

 - Collaborate closely with Support to provide timely updates and clarity on issues.

 - Improve system reliability by identifying root causes, not just patching symptoms.

 - Maintain high quality standards across testing, edge cases, and regressions.
   
   

What We’re Looking For:

 - 2+ years experience as a software engineer

 - Strong written and spoken English fluency

 - Production experience in node.js and react/typescript

 - Experience in fast moving environment, such as small companies or a start-up

 - AI-tooling proficiency: AI as a force multiplier

 - Eligibility to work out of our Mexico City office
   

Nice to have:

 - Experience with global teams

 - Python experience

 - U.S-based startup exposure



What We Offer:

 - Competitive Salary

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Ownership and Opportunity for Advancement

For this role, the estimated annual base salary range is $45,000 - $50,000 USD, depending on experience and qualifications. We believe in compensating fairly and transparently, and we’re happy to provide more detail during the in

source_jobs.title: VP of Finance
source_jobs.description:
About Arketa:

Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses — whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, studios can manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, run automated marketing, and launch fully branded apps and websites. We make it simple for founders to deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint. We’ve raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It’s a unique moment to join us — we’re building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we’re looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.


The Role:

As VP of Finance, you will partner directly with the CEO/co-founder and CTO/co-founder to drive financial strategy, ensure analytical rigor across the company, and help guide Arketa through its next phase of scale. You will sit the intersection of product, growth, and capital strategy, helping translate company ambition into disciplined financial execution.

This is a highly strategic and hands-on role for someone excited to build finance from the ground up while operating as a core thought partner to leadership.

For the first 6 to 12 months, you're the analyst, the modeler, and the strategist.

This role is hybrid to our NYC (NoHo) office.


What You’ll Own:

Financial Strategy & Modeling

 - Own Arketa’s operating model, forecasting, and long-range financial planning

 - Build rigorous revenue and margin models across SaaS, payments, and emerging product lines

 - Lead scenario planning tied to pricing strategy, product launches, and growth investments

 - Establish financial frameworks that enable fast, high-confidence decision making

 - Ensure modeling accuracy and analytical consistency across the business

Strategic Finance & Executive Partnership

 - Act as a strategic partner to the CEO and leadership team

 - Translate company strategy into financial plans and measurable outcomes

 - Support board reporting, investor updates, and potential future fundraising efforts

 - Develop KPI frameworks connecting product adoption, revenue performance, and company health

 - Provide analytical insight that informs major company decisions

Finance Function Buildout

 - Own budgeting and planning processes across departments

 - Partner with external accounting firms on close, reporting, and compliance

 - Design scalable financial processes appropriate for a high-growth SaaS company

 - Improve visibility into unit economics, margins, and operational efficiency

Business Partnership & Growth Initiatives

 - Partner cross-functionally with Product, Sales, and Operations teams

 - Provide financial insight on major investments and strategic initiatives

 - Help leadership prioritize resources based on ROI and long-term impact
   

What We’re Looking For:

 - 7+ years of experience across investment, strategic finance, or high-performance analytical roles, currently operating at a Senior Director, VP, or equivalent level, or ready to step into that scope

 - Highly recommended background
   
   Candidates must have 1–3+ years in at least one of the following:
   
   - Investment Banking (M&A)
   
   - Private Equity or Growth Equity
   
   - Buy-side investing
   
   - Corporate / Internal M&A at a large-scale company

 - Exceptional financial modeling expertise with the ability to independently build and pressure-test complex operating models, forecasts, and scenario analyses

 - Demonstrated ability to translate ambiguous business problems into structured financial frameworks and clear executive decisions

 - Experience partnering closely with founders or executive leadership teams as a strategic advisor

 - Strong intuition for b2b

source_jobs.title: Lead Instructor
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ARKETA

Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses; whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, studios can manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, run automated marketing, and launch fully branded apps and websites. We make it simple for founders to deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint.

We’ve raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It’s a unique moment to join us. We’re building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we’re looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring a Lead Instructor to support the opening and ongoing success of a luxury social wellness club in New York City, as our Flagship to Arketa-powered studio owners.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

We believe in setting our team up to thrive.

First 30 days, Build the foundation: You'll work 1:1 with our CEO to build two opening formats: sculpt and strength and learning Arketa's platform inside and out. You’ll recruit and hire instructors to bring this vision to life.

30–90 days - Build the business: Once the studio is live, the focus shifts to cultivating the community and demonstrating excellence as an instructor. You are continuously iterating and calibrating, laying the groundwork for a thriving studio community.

You will play a critical role in building the foundation of the class experience. Our customers may visit our Flagship and you’ll be the go-to-expert on managing a studio through our state of the art tech.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Partner closely with our CEO building two opening fitness formats

 - Anchoring the early class schedule and driving first-mover memberships

 - Acting as a culture-setter and face of the studio

 - Recruit, hire and train additional instructors through your network

 - Building community through your existing following and Arketa's platform

 - Ensure every interaction reflects brand values and consistency

 - Teach full-time at our Flagship

 - Collaborate with our Partnerships manager for organizing and hosting community and customer events
   
   


WHY THIS ROLE IS SPECIAL

 - You’ll inform and define a studio launch by defining excellence in practice and the team you build

 - You’ll have real ownership, visibility, and impact across the business
   
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - 4+ years as a sculpt, mat Pilates or strength-based instructor

 - You’ve taught other instructors modalities

 - Thrive in ambiguity

 - Culture driver & carrier: you pride yourself as the extension of the studio and modality

 - Highly organized, detail-oriented, and execution-focused

 - Strong communicator who is warm, direct, and practical

 - NYC-based and excited to be onsite and hands-on with this as their full-time teaching role




BONUS

 - You’ve taught strength/functional, barre and yoga + sculpt hybrid modalities



For this role, the estimated annual base salary range is $85,000 - $110,000 USD, depending on experience and qualifications. We believe in compensating fairly and transparently, and we’re happy to provide more detail during the interview process.

Arketa is an equal opportunity employer and is committed to diversity in its workforce. We actively encourage applications from all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We believe that a diverse and inclusive workplace fosters innovation and creativity, and we welcome candidates who share our values of respect, collaboration, and excellence.

source_jobs.title: Director of Customer Care
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ARKETA

Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses, whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, studios can manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, run automated marketing, and launch fully branded apps and websites. We make it simple for founders to deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint. We've raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It's a unique moment to join us. We're building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we're looking for a leader who is excited to help shape what world-class partner support looks like in the wellness category.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Arketa is seeking a Director of Customer Care to lead the support function as we scale. Reporting to the VP of Customer Experience, you'll empower a team of team leaders and the support specialists who report to them. You will drive operational excellence on every partner touchpoint, own the strategic direction of Customer Care, and partner across Sales, Product, and Engineering to continuously improve the platform experience for our partners.

You'll be responsible for the support experience of thousands of wellness operators today and many times that in the years ahead. The ideal candidate is energized by building scalable infrastructure in a high-growth environment, investing deeply in team development as we scale, and setting a high bar for what "best-in-class support" looks like in the wellness category.




ABOUT THE TEAM

Customer Care sits within Arketa's Customer Experience organization, alongside onboarding, data migration, education, and retention. CX is the linchpin of our partner relationship. For many of our partners, the support team is the human face of Arketa.

The Director of Customer Care will shape what this function looks like for the next several years and will be a critical partner to Product, Engineering, Sales, and the broader CX organization.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - This is a builder's role. Expect to be as hands-on as you are strategic for the first six months, designing the playbooks and shaping the team you'll scale.

 - Foster a culture of engagement and excitement to drive partner-centered results, where the team feels empowered to put partners first and develop their careers at Arketa.

 - Empower a team of team leaders and their support specialists, investing in their growth as Arketa scales and building the next generation of CX leadership at the company.

 - Lead the strategic direction of Customer Care for Arketa's partners, including identifying and implementing emerging technologies and AI-enabled workflows that drive operational excellence and exceptional partner outcomes.

 - Own execution of quarterly and annual objectives for the Customer Care function.

 - Lead change management in planning and facilitating organization-wide improvement to the partner experience.

 - Partner closely with Product and Engineering to continuously improve the platform experience by translating partner feedback, recurring escalations, and root-cause analysis into product improvements that prevent the next 100 tickets, not just resolve the last 10.

 - Own relationship and retention management for our highest-stakes partner situations, including VIP accounts, multi-location operators, and churn-risk moments, turning them into retention wins through thoughtful, fast resolution.

 - Solicit regular internal and external partner feedback to lead continuous process improvements to the partner experience.

 - Work cross-functionally with Sales, Onboarding, Account Management, and other CX teams to deliver improvements to the partner experience that go beyond the support

source_jobs.title: Head of Growth Marketing
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses, whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, users can easily manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, and run marketing campaigns via text and email. We also offer fully branded apps and websites, so studios can deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint.

We've raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It's a unique moment to join us. We're building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we're looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.




WHAT IT'S ACTUALLY LIKE TO WORK HERE

We move fast and we expect ownership. The team is mission-obsessed, lean, and unafraid to roll up our sleeves. You'll work directly with our CEO and leadership team on decisions that shape the company. We celebrate wins, learn from misses, and protect each other's energy along the way.




THE ROLE

As Arketa's Head of Growth Marketing, you'll own the full marketing function and lead a team of eight across performance marketing, lifecycle, product marketing, social and community, partnerships, and SDR. You'll be the connective tissue between marketing spend and revenue outcomes, partnering closely with our CEO and other cross-functional partners to build a marketing engine that fuels Arketa's next stage of growth.

This is a category-defining moment. Arketa is the only platform in our space with marketing, referral and gamification, and payroll and accounting all built fully in-house. We're going deep on vertical AI, embedding intelligence across the platform so wellness operators can automate the busywork and focus on growth. We have product market fit and brand awareness compounding fast. We need a leader who can tell that story at scale and build a flywheel that scales us through our next ARR inflection point.

This role is hybrid 4-days to our NYC (NoHo) office.




30-60-90 DAY GOALS

 - First 30 days: Get up to speed on the business, the team, the product, and our customer. Build relationships with each direct report, the CEO, sales leadership, and RevOps. Audit current channel performance and spend.

 - First 60 days: Begin owning the marketing model. Diagnose what's working and what isn't across acquisition, lifecycle, and brand. Surface clear recommendations on budget allocation by channel.

 - First 90 days: Fully manage the team. Have a defined point of view on budget and spend by channel, an owned forecasting and reporting model, and a hiring plan for the function.
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own Arketa's marketing strategy end to end, from positioning and brand through paid acquisition, lifecycle, and partnerships

 - Lead, develop, and grow a team of four across performance marketing, product marketing, lifecycle, social and community, partnerships and events, plus a roster of fractional creative and channel specialists

 - Leverage AI to scale the marketing function's output and efficiency before scaling headcount, building a team that punches well above its weight through smart tooling and automation

 - Tie every marketing motion back to pipeline and revenue, partnering closely with Sales and RevOps to drive quality demos and accelerate close rates

 - Manage and allocate the marketing budget with confidence, balancing smart, bullish spend in high-conviction channels with disciplined cuts where ROI is unclear

 - Own the marketing forecasting model and report progress against pipeline, ARR, and efficiency metrics to the CEO and broader leadership team

 - Partner with the CEO and leadership team on Arketa's expansion from SMB into mid-market

 - Build the playbook, hiring plan, a

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative, UK
source_jobs.description:
Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses—whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, users can easily manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, and run marketing campaigns via text and email. We also offer fully branded apps and websites, so studios can deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint.

We’ve raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It’s a unique moment to join us—we’re building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we’re looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.



The Role:

As a Sales Development Representative (SDR), you’ll be the first point of contact for prospective customers, using creative outreach methods to introduce them to Arketa and demonstrate how we can help their business thrive.

You’ll focus on small and mid-sized wellness studios & entrepreneurs, driving outbound sales through high volume phone calls (4-6 hours per day), emails, social media messaging, and other innovative approaches. Your goal will be to build the initial connections with potential clients while managing your pipeline meticulously.

If you thrive on connecting with people, love fitness, and are eager to grow your career, this is your chance to make an impact.



Your 30/60/90 Expectations:

 - 30 Days: Generate our minimum of SQLs. Gain product, knowledge, meet the team and refine your sales skills

 - 60 days: Double our minimum of SQLs and push towards higher volume.

 - 90 days: Achieve the full monthly SQL quota, and this is just your launching pad…



What You’ll Do:

 - Connect with interested prospects: Reach out to leads from trials, events, content downloads, and marketing campaigns via phone, email, and SMS

 - Qualify and prioritize leads: Understand each prospect's business needs, timeline, and decision-making process to determine their fit with Arketa

 - Schedule high-quality demos: Set up meetings between qualified prospects and Account Executives, ensuring smooth handoffs with complete context

 - Become an Arketa expert: Understand our platform inside and out so you can speak confidently about how we solve real problems for fitness businesses

 - Keep everything organized: Maintain accurate records in HubSpot, track lead progression, and ensure nothing falls through the cracks

 - Collaborate across teams: Work closely with Marketing to optimize lead quality and provide feedback on campaign performance

 - Follow up strategically: Stay connected with prospects who aren't ready to move forward immediately, nurturing them until they're ready for next steps

What We’re Looking For:

 - Fitness Enthusiast: You’ve worked in a fitness studio and understand the ins and outs of studio software (bonus if you’ve used Arketa, Mindbody, Wellnessliving, Marianatek, or Momence).

 - Sales Talent Potential: You’re ready to dive into outbound efforts using phone, email, messaging, and creative outreach.

 - Strong Communicator: You can easily connect with people, articulate ideas clearly in both written and verbal communication, and excel at building rapport.

 - Grit & Determination: You’re someone who doesn’t shy away from the challenge of persistence and knows that each conversation, whether immediate or down the road, brings you closer to making a real impact. You thrive on connecting with people and are excited to overcome obstacles to drive results.

 - Organized & Driven: You can manage multiple priorities—whether it’s tracking leads, emails, calls, or social outreach—and you’re resourceful in problem-solving to meet business needs. You’ll keep your work updated in hubspot and our sales software tools.

 - Mission

source_jobs.title: Customer Care Representative

We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses—whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, users can easily manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, and run marketing campaigns via text and email. We also offer fully branded apps and websites, so studios can deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint.

We’ve raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It’s a unique moment to join us—we’re building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we’re looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Lovers of fitness and advocates for wellness businesses wanted! Our Customer Experience team is the first line of support for the studios, gyms, and wellness providers who run their business on Arketa. We answer their questions, solve real operational problems, and represent the voice of our Partners internally to shape the product. If you love building relationships, making people smile, and increasing access to wellness around the world, we want to meet you. Bonus points if you've already used Arketa (or a comparable platform) inside a wellness business yourself.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Customer Support Advocate, you'll act as the single point of contact for our Partners on inbound support inquiries, owning each interaction from first message to resolution. You'll guide Partners through Arketa's features, move quickly to creatively solve problems, and act as their internal advocate to influence our product decisions and roadmap. You'll address real operational problems for real wellness businesses — schedule issues, payment questions, migration blockers, integration hiccups — and deliver an exceptional experience that builds trust and loyalty to Arketa.

This is a fast-paced, high-volume support environment. You'll work across chat, email, and Zoom each day and manage multiple conversations in parallel.

You will work remotely but be expected to support local studios on an as needed basis in Austin, TX.




HERE'S WHAT YOU'LL DO DAY-TO-DAY

 - Own each Partner interaction start to finish across chat, email, and scheduled Zoom calls — building trust, rapport, and partnership along the way.

 - Show up for your shift and your Partners. You'll work scheduled hours and stay available and engaged with the queue during that time. The pace is steady, the volume is real, and your Partners are counting on you being there when they reach out.

 - Own the resolution, not just the response. You're accountable for solving the Partner's problem the first time wherever possible — not just replying quickly. That means digging into the actual issue, confirming the fix landed, and closing the loop so the Partner doesn't have to come back for the same thing twice.

 - Think creatively, pivot fast, operate in ambiguity, and collaborate strategically across stakeholders. You'll problem-solve across the full surface area of the Arketa product, working with Product, Engineering, Onboarding, and Migrations to unblock Partners on issues that don't have a clean playbook yet.

 - Unlock the full power of Arketa for every Partner. Solve real operational problems, customize solutions to the way their business actually runs, and turn Partners into our biggest fans through expertise, empathy, and follow-through.

 - Become and stay an Arketa expert. Continuously learn new features, leverage AI-assisted internal tools to accelerate your research and drafting, and own the accuracy and Arketa voice of every response that goes out under your name.

 - Advocate for our Partners internally. Bring insights and industry feedback back to Product, Engineering,

source_jobs.title: Sales Account Executive - SMB, UK
source_jobs.description:
Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses—whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, users can easily manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, and run marketing campaigns via text and email. We also offer fully branded apps and websites, so studios can deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint.

We’ve raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It’s a unique moment to join us—we’re building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we’re looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.



The Role: 

As an SMB Account Executive, you’ll help our studio customers understand the value of our all-in-one solution. You will develop new and lasting relationships in a variety of accounts. You’ll join a hardworking, high-energy, fast paced inside sales team and will advance deals through the sales cycle in collaboration with our product & eng team, onboarding, and customer success team.

You’ll focus on small and mid-sized studio businesses. You will need to excel at outbound sales efforts and track your pipeline meticulously. Strong organization & communication skills are key, as you will be the first face of arketa to many prospective customers.



What You’ll Do:

 - Strong lead generation skills sourcing net new deals

 - Own the entire sales & upsell process from prospecting to close for new and existing arketa customers

 - Meet and exceed quarterly sales targets

 - Maintain accurate pipeline management with expert-level forecasting

 - Execute outbound sales efforts with excellence, fearlessness & enthusiasm via email, social media, cold calling, -gifting, in-person visits and more

 - Understand customer needs and requirements

 - Strong product demonstration skills: showcase how the arketa technology will add value to prospective customers and their unique use cases

 - Communicate forecasted deal flow & contract terms regularly to internal team to ensure a delightful onboarding experience for customers

 - Be an exemplary ambassador for arketa, both internally and externally

 - Be a team player and work with other sellers internally



What We’re Looking For:

 - 3+ years of technology sales experience with a preference for SMB SaaS. You should have a sales methodology that you like and have used before! (Sandler, etc)

 - Ideally experience selling to restaurants, fitness studios or small businesses

 - Ability to prioritize multiple emails, phone calls, social media and more

 - Thrives in a fast-paced, diverse, and highly collaborative team environment.

 - You have a deep understanding of outbound selling and prospecting.

 - You understand a relationship-led, sales motion and have demonstrated a track record of exceeding quotas & selling success.

 - You are a strong written and verbal communicator.

 - You are passionate about our overall mission and how arketa can help our customers

 - You are scrappy, resourceful and a creative problem solver when discovering the business needs of your customer and understanding how arketa plugs into the bigger picture.

 - You know how, or will learn prior to starting how to, use hubspot to track & forecast sales pipeline

 - Experience selling to fitness studios a huge plus

 - BA/BS degree preferred - Must be authorized to work in the U.S.



What We Offer: 

 - Competitive Salary, Stock Options, and Performance-based Bonuses

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Ownership and Opportunity for Advancement

For this role, the estimated annual base salary range is up to $45,000 to $60,000 USD, depending on experience and qualifications. In addition to base pay, we offer equity and performance-based bon

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
Arketa is building the operating system for modern fitness and wellness. Our mission is to make wellness more accessible by empowering studios and wellness entrepreneurs to grow beyond in-person classes. We provide a powerful, all-in-one platform to manage and scale hybrid wellness businesses—whether online, in-person, or both.

With Arketa, users can easily manage schedules, accept bookings and payments, build on-demand video libraries, host retreats, and run marketing campaigns via text and email. We also offer fully branded apps and websites, so studios can deliver a seamless, professional experience across every customer touchpoint.

We’ve raised $15M in Series A funding and are growing quickly. It’s a unique moment to join us—we’re building a category-defining company in a fast-moving space, and we’re looking for teammates who are excited to help shape the future of wellness.



The Role: 

As a Sales Development Representative (SDR), you’ll be the first point of contact for prospective customers, using creative outreach methods to introduce them to Arketa and demonstrate how we can help their business thrive.

You’ll focus on small and mid-sized wellness studios & entrepreneurs, driving outbound sales through phone calls, emails, social media messaging, and other innovative approaches. Your goal will be to build the initial connections with potential clients while managing your pipeline meticulously.

If you thrive on connecting with people, love fitness, and are eager to grow your career into an Account Executive (AE) role, this is your chance to make an impact.



What You’ll Do:

 - Connect with interested prospects: Reach out to leads from trials, events, content downloads, and marketing campaigns via phone, email, and SMS

 - Qualify and prioritize leads: Understand each prospect's business needs, timeline, and decision-making process to determine their fit with Arketa

 - Schedule high-quality demos: Set up meetings between qualified prospects and Account Executives, ensuring smooth handoffs with complete context

 - Become an Arketa expert: Understand our platform inside and out so you can speak confidently about how we solve real problems for fitness businesses

 - Keep everything organized: Maintain accurate records in HubSpot, track lead progression, and ensure nothing falls through the cracks

 - Collaborate across teams: Work closely with Marketing to optimize lead quality and provide feedback on campaign performance

 - Follow up strategically: Stay connected with prospects who aren't ready to move forward immediately, nurturing them until they're ready for next steps

What We’re Looking For:

 - Has some cold-calling experience, ideally in SMB SaaS, selling to fitness studios, restaurants, or small businesses. Recruiting and real estate experience are also particularly relevant

 - Familiarity with Hubspot, Nooks (or a parallel dialer), Instagram, Loom, Vidyard and lead sources

 - BA/BS degree preferred but not required

 - Fitness Enthusiast: You’ve worked in a fitness studio and understand the ins and outs of studio software (bonus if you’ve used Arketa, Mindbody, Wellnessliving, Marianatek, or Momence).

 - Sales Talent: You’re ready to dive into outbound efforts using phone, email, messaging, and creative outreach.

 - Strong Communicator: You can easily connect with people, articulate ideas clearly in both written and verbal communication, and excel at building rapport.

 - Grit & Determination: You’re someone who doesn’t shy away from the challenge of persistence and knows that each conversation, whether immediate or down the road, brings you closer to making a real impact. You thrive on connecting with people and are excited to overcome obstacles to drive results.

 - Organized & Driven: You can manage multiple priorities—whether it’s tracking leads, emails, calls, or social outreach—and you’re resourceful in problem-solving to meet business needs. You’ll keep your work updated in hubspot and our sa
